title: Conventions block schema underspecified for relational / lens / lookup discoverability — flat slug-id rules with no category / applies_to / related_decisions / title / lifecycle / source
description: Conventions block (.project/schemas/conventions.schema.json + .project/conventions.json) was authored pre-DEC-0013 + pre-lens-infrastructure. Schema item shape: id (freeform slug like 'esm' / 'tsc-build' / 'no-pi-dir'), description (single payload), enforcement (lint/test/review/manual), severity (error/warning/info). Missing facets that the surrounding lens / relational / lookup canon requires: (1) no category / domain field — LLMs cannot lens 'filing conventions' vs 'naming conventions' vs 'commit conventions' vs 'build conventions'; 16 entries are flat-list. (2) No applies_to field — no surface scoping (substrate / source / tests / commit / specific-package). (3) No related_decisions — conventions emerging from DECs have no closure-table edge home; relations.json cannot carry the linkage canonically. (4) No title field separate from description — listings cannot render compactly; no scan-friendly facet for filter-block-items. (5) Id is freeform slug, no canonical prefix pattern — cannot pattern-match by lens bin shape; cross-block id resolution inconsistent. (6) No status / lifecycle — conventions accumulate without supersession or retirement tracking. (7) No source / citation field — conventions don't cite the file or DEC that enforces them. (8) No canonical_vocabulary field (cf. FGAP schema). Same shape question applies to other zero-item or early-authored blocks: architecture, domain, requirements — those schemas may equally predate the relational layer + lens infrastructure that DEC-0013 + lens-view.ts + project-context.ts established.

evidence.reference: Item shape: id / description / enforcement / severity — no category / applies_to / related_decisions / title / status / source / canonical_vocabulary
16 flat-list rules; first 3: 'esm' / 'tsc-build' / 'no-pi-dir' — slug ids, no facets

Comparison: FGAP has title / status / priority / package / layer / canonical_vocabulary / impact / evidence / proposed_resolution / related_features / related_decisions / related_issues — facet-rich shape
Comparison: DEC has title / status / context / decision / consequences / supersedes / superseded_by / related_findings / related_features / related_gaps / references — also facet-rich
Lens projection assumes items have facets for bin grouping
filterBlockItems / walkAncestors / findReferences operate on facet-bearing items

impact: LLM looking up 'how to file substrate' or 'what build commands to use' must grep description strings — no facet supports the query. filter-block-items predicate cannot bin conventions by domain. walk-ancestors cannot traverse from a DEC to its enacted convention rule (no edge field). Cross-block discoverability of conventions is degraded compared to facet-rich blocks. The same underspecification likely affects architecture / domain / requirements schemas (all zero-item; authored pre-relational-layer). Affects any LLM trying to enter project context via conventions-block as a query entry point.
proposed_resolution: Schema-migrate conventions.schema.json to add: title (scan-friendly summary), category (substrate-filing / naming / commit / build / monitoring / etc.), applies_to (target surface), status (active / retired / superseded), related_decisions (closure-table edge home via relations.json), source (file path or DEC citation), canonical_vocabulary (engineering terminology). Migrate existing 16 rules to populate new fields. Similar audit + migration for architecture / domain / requirements / project schemas — surface their relational/lens/lookup gaps via this FGAP's resolution sweep. Cross-decision with FGAP-047 vocabulary settlement (display-name + categorization) and FGAP-048 id-prefix drift (canonical prefix CONV- vs continued freeform slug).
